title: Each mode requires different fields — wrong combo is a 422 at submit, not an empty result
summary: spiderCompanyData has one endpoint and three modes with different required fields, enforced by the payload validator before dispatch: search needs name; lookup needs identifier AND country; vat needs vat_number (or identifier). TWO failure layers: (1) schema 422 (missing required field, no job_id, never ran); (2) worker success:false after a well-formed request (HTTP 200, status completed) — e.g. lookup with country:DE passes the schema but the worker returns success:false 'Unsupported country: DE', or 'Company not found'. The schema validates field PRESENCE, not the country VALUE, so a bad country is a worker-level success:false, NOT a 422 — only visible by reading data.success. Mode defaults to search, so an identifier with no mode is a nameless search -> 422. Have a name but no ID: search first to get source_id, then lookup by id+country.
references: windmill-scripts/spidercompanydata_lookup.py:422-426 — worker rejects non-GB/US country at runtime with success:false 'Unsupported country'
windmill-scripts/spidercompanydata_lookup.py:428-433 — not-found -> success:false 'Company not found' (posted to /complete, HTTP 200)
